heading: Thursday Night
text: Snow or wintry mix near and south of Route 4, and a chance north to Route 2 after midnight; a glaze of ice possible, and a trace to 2 inches of snow and sleet, with locally higher amounts in the southern mountains. Lows from the upper teens to mid 20s east, mid 20s to around 30 west. Winds becoming east 5 to 10 mph.

heading: Friday
text: Mostly cloudy. Any snow or mix south of Route 2 tapering off early, and possibly lingering until mid morning southern New Hampshire; little or no additional accumulation. Highs from the mid 30s to around 40, with some lower 30s in New Hampshire, and low to mid 40s in the St. Lawrence Valley. Winds becoming southeast 5 to 15 mph, and gusting to 25 mph in the Champlain Valley.

heading: Friday Night
text: Mostly cloudy, with a chance of light rain or freezing rain from the Adirondacks and Route 4 northward. Lows in the upper 20s to mid 30s. South winds 5 to 10 mph, and gusting to 30 mph in the Champlain and St. Lawrence valleys.
